title: A smart mirror mount device
abstract: Techniques, systems, and devices are disclosed for manipulating light beams using one or more mirror mount assemblies communicatively coupled through a communication channel to similar devices or an external device for sensing purposes, providing user interface, or connection to the internet cloud. This mirror mount assembly provides movement of a mechanical mount and further includes an actuator, a driver electrically coupled to the actuator to drive the actuator; a control module electrically coupled to the driver to provide drive signals to the driver, a position sensor, and a control module including a processor for generating the drive signals and for autonomously implementing user defined functions on the mirror mount assembly. The mirror mount assembly also includes one or more communication interfaces coupled to the control module to receive one or more input signals from external devices. The external interface devices can include optical sensors and user interfaces.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A motorized apparatus for manipulating light, comprising: a first mirror mount assembly that includes: a first mirror; a first mechanical mount supporting the first mirror; and a first motorized drive module coupled to the first mechanical mount to control rotation and position of the first mirror, wherein the first motorized drive module includes a first communication interface for communications with a device outside the first mirror mount assembly; a second mirror mount assembly that includes: a second mirror; a second mechanical mount supporting the second mirror; and a second motorized drive module coupled to the second mechanical mount to control rotation and position of the second mirror, wherein the second motorized drive module includes a second communication interface for communications with a device outside the second mirror mount assembly; and a communication channel coupled between the first communication interface and the second communication interface to communicate information between the first mirror mount assembly and the second mirror mount assembly in directing a light beam from a light source to a desired light destination using the first and second mirror mount assemblies. 2. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, wherein the information for directing a light beam includes information for determining a distance between the first mirror mount assembly and the second mirror mount assembly. 3. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first motorized drive module further includes: a first actuator mechanically coupled to the first mechanical mount; a first driver electrically coupled to the first actuator to drive the first actuator; and a first controller electrically coupled to the first driver to provide drive signals to the first driver; and 

 wherein the second motorized drive module further includes: a second actuator mechanically coupled to the second mechanical mount; a second driver electrically coupled to the second actuator the second actuator; and a second controller electrically coupled to the second driver to provide drive signals to the second driver. 4. The motorized apparatus of claim 3, wherein each of the first actuator and the second actuator is a stepper motor, DC motor, or a piezoelectric actuator. 5. The motorized apparatus of claim 3, wherein the first motorized drive module further includes a first sensor coupled to the first mechanical mount to detect a rotational or positional change of the first mechanical mount, wherein the first sensor is electrically coupled to the first controller to provide feedback for adjusting angle and position of the first mirror. 6. The motorized apparatus of claim 5, wherein the second motorized drive module is configured to receive sensing signals from the first sensor through the communication channel. 7. The motorized apparatus of claim 3, wherein the second motorized drive module further includes a second sensor coupled to the second mechanical mount to detect a rotational or positional change of the second mechanical mount, wherein the second sensor is electrically coupled to the second controller to provide feedback for adjusting angle and position of the second mirror. 8. The motorized apparatus of claim 7, wherein the first motorized drive module is configured to receive sensing signals from the second sensor through the communication channel. 9. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, wherein the communication channel is wireless communication channel which includes a Bluetooth channel or a WiFi channel. 10. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, wherein the communication channel is wired communication bus which includes a Universal Serial Bus (USB), a UART, a RS232, or an Ethernet. 11. The motorized apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the first mirror mount assembly and the second mirror mount assembly communicate with each other through the communication channel to determine a desired path for the light beam without a user's intervention. 12. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a user interface coupled to the communication channel, wherein the user interface is configured to allow a user to control the first and the second mirror mount assemblies through the communication channel. 13. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, wherein the user interface includes a personal computer, a mobile phone, or a tablet computer. 14. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a feedback mechanism located near the desired light destination to provide a feedback signal based on the light beam at the desired light destination to the first and second mirror mount assemblies. 15. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first and second mirror mount assemblies are configured to collaborate with each other to perform at least one of the following operations on the light beam: light beam modification by redirection; light beam stabilization; or light beam path alignment. 16. The motorized apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first and second integrated mirror mount assemblies are configured to collaborate with each other to enable a wide angle range of adjustment in directing the light beam. 17. A system for manipulation of light, comprising: a mirror mount assembly that includes: a mirror; a mechanical mount supporting the mirror; and 

 a motorized control module coupled to the mechanical mount to control rotation and position of the mirror, wherein the motorized control module includes a communication interface; an external control device including an user interface for allowing a user to control the integrated mirror mount assembly; and a communication channel coupled between the communication interface and the external control device to communicate information between the mirror mount assembly and the external control device in directing a light beam from a light source to a desired light destination using the mirror. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the external control device includes a personal computer, a mobile phone, a tablet computer, or other mobile computing devices. 19. The system of claim 17, wherein the system further includes an external sensor integrated with an electronic feedback which is coupled to the motorized control module, wherein the external sensor is configured to receive a reflected light from the mirror to detect an effect of the mirror on the light beam, and feedback the detected signal to the motorized control module. 20. The system of claim 19, wherein the external sensor is configured to measure an optical power, a spectrum, a beam profile or quality, a pulse width, a beam position, or other optical properties. 21. The system of claim 19, wherein the motorized drive module further includes: an actuator mechanically coupled to the mechanical mount; a driver electrically coupled to the actuator to drive the actuator; and a controller electrically coupled to the driver to provide drive signals to the driver. 22. The system of claim 21, wherein the motorized drive module further includes an integrated sensor coupled to the mechanical mount to detect a rotational or positional change of the mechanical mount, wherein the integrated sensor is electrically coupled to the controller to provide feedback for adjusting angle and position of the mirror. 23. The system of claim 17, wherein the actuator is a stepper motor, DC motor, or a piezoelectric actuator. 24. The system of claim 17, wherein the communication channel is wireless communication channel which includes a Bluetooth channel or a WiFi channel. 25. The system of claim 17, wherein the communication channel is wired communication bus which includes a Universal Serial Bus (USB), a UART, a RS232, or an Ethernet. 26. The system of claim 17, wherein the mirror mount assembly includes an angular position sensor to detect a rotational motion of the mechanical mount based on measuring a capacitance change in a capacitor formed by two metal patterns as one of the metal patterns rotates with respect to the other metal pattern. 27. The system of claim 17, wherein the mirror mount assembly includes a linear position sensor to detect a linear motion of the mechanical mount based on measuring a capacitance change in a capacitor formed by two metal patterns as one of the metal patterns displaces with respect to the other metal pattern. 28. A method for manipulating a light beam, comprising: directing a light beam from a light source onto a first mirror mount assembly; redirecting the light beam from the first mirror mount assembly onto a second mirror mount assembly; and redirecting the light beam from the second mirror mount assembly toward a desired light destination, wherein the first mirror mount assembly and the second mirror mount assembly are coupled by a communication channel to communicate information through the communication channel and collaborate with each other to direct the light beam from the light source to the desired light destination. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein redirecting the light beam from the first mirror mount assembly onto the second mirror mount assembly includes using a user defined program stored in the first mirror mount assembly to redirect the light beam. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein redirecting the light beam from the second mirror mount assembly toward the desired light destination includes using a user defined program stored in the second mirror mount assembly to redirect the light beam. 31. The method of claim 28, wherein redirecting the light beam from the first mirror mount assembly onto the second mirror mount assembly includes allowing a user to control the first mirror mount assembly through an external control device coupled to the communication channel. 32. The method of claim 31 , wherein redirecting the light beam from the second mirror mount assembly toward the desired light destination includes allowing a user to control the second mirror mount assembly through the external control device coupled to the communication channel. 33. The method of claim 31, wherein the external control device includes a personal computer, a mobile phone, a tablet computer, or other mobile computing devices. 34. The method of claim 31 , wherein the first mirror mount assembly that includes: a first mirror; a first mechanical mount supporting the first mirror; and a first motorized drive module coupled to the first mechanical mount to control rotation and position of the first mirror, wherein the first motorized drive module includes a first communication interface; and the second mirror mount assembly that includes: a second mirror; a second mechanical mount supporting the second mirror; and a second motorized drive module coupled to the second mechanical mount to control rotation and position of the second mirror, wherein the second motorized drive module includes a second communication interface, wherein the communication channel is coupled between the first communication interface and the second communication interface to communicate information between the first mirror mount assembly and the second mirror mount assembly in directing a light beam 

 from a light source to a desired light destination using the first and second integrated mirror mount assemblies. 35. A mirror mount assembly for manipulation of light, comprising: a mirror; a mechanical mount supporting the mirror; an actuator mechanically coupled to the mechanical mount to provide a movement of the mechanical mount; a driver electrically coupled to the actuator to drive the actuator; and a control module electrically coupled to the driver to provide drive signals to the driver, wherein the control module includes a processor for generating the drive signals and for autonomously implementing user defined functions on the mirror mount assembly. 36. The mirror mount assembly of claim 35, wherein the mirror mount assembly includes one or more communication interfaces coupled to the control module to receive one or more input signals from a first set of external devices and to transmit one or more output signals to a second set of external devices. 37. The mirror mount assembly of claim 36, wherein the one or more communication interfaces include one or more digital interfaces to couple to power sensors, position sensitive detectors, knobs for position control, or Blue Tooth or WiFi interfaces. 38. The mirror mount assembly of claim 36, wherein the one or more communication interfaces include one or more USB interfaces to couple to cameras, USB controlled instruments such as spectrometers, or WiFi adaptors. 39. The mirror mount assembly of claim 38, wherein the USB interfaces are configured to control one or more USB devices plugged into USB ports by loading proper software drivers for the one or more USB devices. 40. The mirror mount assembly of claim 35, wherein the processor includes a single core CPU or a multi-core CPU, 41. The mirror mount assembly of claim 37, wherein the processor includes a field- programmable gate array (FPGA) 42. The mirror mount assembly of claim 41, wherein the FPGA is reconfigurable to control the one or more digital interfaces. 43. The mirror mount assembly of claim 36, wherein the mirror mount assembly is a master mirror mount assembly, and wherein the control module in the master mirror mount assembly is configured to control one or more slave mirror mount assemblies through the one or more communication interfaces. 44. The mirror mount assembly of claim 36, wherein the mirror mount assembly is configured to communicate with an external user interface, an Internet cloud, or a laboratory instrument through the one or more communication interfaces. 45. The mirror mount assembly of claim 35, wherein the movement of the mechanical mount includes an angular movement or a linear movement. 46. The mirror mount assembly of claim 35, wherein the actuator is a stepper motor, DC motor, or a piezoelectric actuator. 47. The mirror mount assembly of claim 35, further including an integrated sensor coupled to the mechanical mount to detect an angular or linear position change of the mechanical mount, wherein the integrated sensor is electrically coupled to the control module to provide feedback signals to be processed by the control module for adjusting angle and position of the mirror. 48. The mirror mount assembly of claim 47, wherein the integrated sensor is attached directly to the mechanical mount so as to be independent of drive mechanics of the driver and any inaccuracy of operation of the actuator. 49. The mirror mount assembly of claim 47, wherein the integrated sensor is a patterned capacitive sensor that includes: 

 alternating periodic pattern of metal lines or metal rings formed on a moving board; and a mating pattern formed on a stationary board that has a gap from the moving board, wherein the alternating periodic pattern and the mating pattern form a capacitance, wherein the capacitive sensor is configured to measure a change in the capacitance when the moving board rotates or displaces with respect to the stationary board; and wherein the change in the capacitance is used to determine an angular or linear position change between the moving board and the stationary board. 50. The mirror mount assembly of claim 47, further includes a stabilization mechanism which is configured to use feedback from internal and external sensors to adjust a position of the mirror mount assembly to compensate for environmental effects. 51. The mirror mount assembly of claim 50, wherein the stabilization mechanism is configured to use the feedback from the internal temperature sensors to stabilize a temperature of the mirror mount assembly. 52. The mirror mount assembly of claim 51, where the temperature of the mirror mount assembly is maintained by an internal heater controlled by the processor. 53. The mirror mount assembly of claim 51 , where the temperature of the mirror mount assembly is maintained by the processor operating at higher and lower power efficiency modes, such as by adding extra computing cycles, or reading and writing data to a memory, to increase thermal dissipation as needed for stabilization of temperature. 54. The mirror mount assembly of claim 35, further includes a sensor for sensing other elements in the environment, wherein information from the sensor is used to aid in defining locations to direct the beam. 55. The mirror mount assembly of claim 54, wherein the mirror mount assembly uses one or two camera images, along with focus and parallax information, on a moving platform to determine location of other elements in the environment. 56. The mirror mount assembly of Claim 55, wherein the mirror mount assembly uses an ultrasound beam on the moving platform to determine location of other elements in the environment with sonar sensing. 57. The mirror mount assembly of claim 47, wherein the mirror mount assembly uses a guide laser directed onto the mirror as a defined position and angle for providing a reference alignment angle for measuring other positions located relative to the guide laser by using the integrated sensor. 58. A method for manipulating a light beam, comprising: directing a light beam from a light source onto a mirror mount assembly which includes: a mirror; a mechanical mount supporting the mirror; an actuator mechanically coupled to the mechanical mount to provide a movement of the mechanical mount; a driver electrically coupled to the actuator to drive the actuator; and a control module electrically coupled to the driver to provide drive signals to the driver, wherein the control module includes a processor for generating the drive signals and for autonomously implementing user defined functions on the mirror mount assembly; generating a control signal by the control module; redirecting the light beam from the mirror mount assembly toward a desired light destination by controlling the mechanical mount using the control signal. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein generating the control signal by the control module includes using a user defined program stored in the control module to generate the control signal. 60. The method of claim 58, wherein generating the control signal by the control module involves using a feedback signal received from an external sensor coupled to the mirror mount assembly to generate the control signal. 61. A capacitive sensor for determining an angular or linear position change, comprising: 

 alternating periodic pattern of metal lines or metal rings formed on a moving board; and a mating pattern formed on a stationary board that has a gap from the moving board, wherein the alternating periodic pattern and the mating pattern form a capacitance; wherein the capacitive sensor is configured to measure a change in the capacitance when the moving board rotates or displaces with respect to the stationary board; and wherein the measured change in the capacitance is used to determine an angular or linear position change between the moving board and the stationary board. 62. The capacitive sensor of claim 61, wherein the metal rings are configured with two interleaved patterns that are symmetric along the rotation or length of the sensor so that the angular or linear position is determined independent of a distance or a tilt angle between the rotating board and the stationary board. 63. The capacitive sensor of claim 61, further comprising a non-symmetric mating pattern which is used to determine an absolute position in combination with the alternating periodic patterns and the mating pattern. 64. The capacitive sensor of claim 61, wherein the alternating periodic pattern and the mating pattern are high frequency patterns used for increased accuracy. 65. The capacitive sensor of claim 64, wherein the high frequency patterns are shifted 90 degrees in the periodic phase, or quadrature, so when one pattern is at a peak or minimum capacitance, the other quadrature pattern is rapidly varying, to allow high accuracy position or angle determination at all points of movement. 66. The capacitive sensor of claim 61, wherein the change in the capacitance is processed by a processer coupled to the capacitive sensor to determine the angular or linear position change from the absolute position pattern, the increasing frequency patterns, and the patterns at quadrature phase. 67. The capacitive sensor of claim 66, wherein the angular or linear position change is determined from a combination of heuristic algorithms and look up tables within the processor.
